{"key_event":"Congo Free State concessionary rule begins","reasoning":"The Congo Free State established coercive authority around trade posts, armed agents, treaties, and emerging concession systems, but its territorial administration remained uneven and thin beyond major routes. Indigenous societies retained local organization in many areas yet lacked unified, autonomous institutions able to constrain the colonial state, and resistance was fragmented and brutally suppressed.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":2.0,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":4.0}
